checklist.claim: the inventory's exclusion table is complete over its stated population
checklist.evidence: iteration-1's widened population sentence pulled in five module-driven entry points the table never lists; narrowed to what run.sh drives directly or via test_python_scripts.py, and rows added for cloud_writer_deps.py and regenerate-artifacts.py. Re-enumerated mechanically: every member of the narrowed population is now in one of the two tables.

checklist.claim: the retired convention survives on no surface
checklist.evidence: the sweep carried only framing clauses, so the retired OBLIGATION sentence could be re-added alongside the new prose with every pin green; three obligation spellings added. Nine literals x six files now sweep to zero.

checklist.claim: every published budget figure is live-measured
checklist.evidence: six token cells were derived by delta arithmetic rather than the doc's own round(words*1.3); recomputed, and a formula check over the whole table now reports zero drifting rows

phase3_findings.description: The inventory population sentence widened by iteration 1 is false: five module-driven lib/test/*.py entry points sit inside the declared population and appear in neither table
The cloud_writer_contract.py row's iteration-1 annotation ('not driven from run.sh at all') is false — run.sh:50917 drives test_python_scripts.py, which drives the guard
The new #591 routing caveat directs the reader to a coverage-map files entry that cannot exist — lib/test/ is an exempt subtree
The reworked positive control's comment claims it detects a mistyped literal; it cannot, because the planting and counting loops read the same array
The claim-gating clause — the half that makes the non-gated push safe — ships unpinned on all five surfaces while its weaker siblings on the same paragraph are mutation-pinned
The sweep omits the retired rule's operative obligation sentence, so the retired obligation can be re-added alongside the new prose with every pin green (the framing-only-pin hole, #171/#232)
The CONTRIBUTING.md sweep arm added in iteration 1 is inert — that file's retired form is a sentence none of the swept literals carry — while its comment claims it stops a revert
Six diff-modified token cells violate the counting formula the same document states (updated by delta arithmetic rather than round(words*1.3))
The sweep block header still enumerates five surfaces after this change made it six
The reflection-routing case and the forward reference binding it to the obligation are unpinned (dangling-deference class)
CONTRIBUTING.md's Submitting-changes step states the parallel rule without the claim gate the other five surfaces carry
GNU-only \b in a zero-expecting absence pin: on BSD grep the alternation degrades and the pin can only ever count 0, passing on a macOS desk while failing on Linux CI

The overview's 'whole-tree meta-guards stay in the monolith' is false for cloud_writer_contract.py by the inventory's own row; the new reflection bullet names no reflection kind; AC4's mid-iteration scoping is undeclared

prompt_addenda: provenance failure (reviewed-diff check): .devflow/prompt-extensions/review-and-fix.md is present in this run's cached changed-file list, so the loaded consumer extension text is NOT provenance-clean. The extension was loaded and honored as required, but its text is a recorded prompt addendum, so this pass cannot produce an attested clean result and Decide outcome 1 is unreachable by construction. The local-status check passed (the file is tracked and unmodified); it is the reviewed-diff check that fails, because this PR edits the extension that governs this loop.

phase3_findings.description: The retired-convention sweep arm 'Before a commit, phase completion, push, or completion claim, run' is unmatchable by construction: at the pre-#707 baseline that sentence was line-wrapped, so the literal counts 0 across all six swept files while every other literal counts 1. The obligation arm added to close the framing-only-pin hole is itself inert — the #375 wrapped-literal class reintroduced inside the guard written to prevent it. The positive control is structurally blind to it, because it plants the literal as a single line and then finds it in that planted form.
phase3_findings.orchestrator_verified: CONFIRMED by direct execution: the full literal counts 0 at 607ec800 across all six files; the on-line span 'Before a commit, phase completion, push, or' counts 1 in implement.md; the other eight literals each count 1.

phase3_findings.description: The new module has no runs_green_through_the_real_runner test, unlike all five pre-existing modules. It is the first module to run git init and multiple python3 heredocs in a module body and to allocate under DEVFLOW_MODULE_OWNED_SCRATCH_ROOT, so it is the one most likely to behave differently under the focused runner than under the full-suite boundary. Such a test would also convert the registry floor from a minimum into an exact-tally assertion.
phase3_findings.orchestrator_verified: CONFIRMED by direct execution: five runs_green_through_the_real_runner tests exist (repository, review-and-fix-contract, create-issue-contract, capability-profiles, installer-wiring); none names harness-python-guards.

phase3_findings.description: The claim-gating clause states a guard the artifact gives the agent no mechanism to observe. Before this change the full suite was a precondition of the push, so the guard was self-enforcing; the diff removes that serialization and replaces it with a purely observational rule, without instructing the run to capture the concurrent launch to a named file, check its launch status, or record an evidence marker. 'A run that never started' has no observable artifact by construction, so the natural terminal state of a refused launch is pushed-nothing-to-read-claim-made — with every mutation pin green because the sentence is intact.

'no focused module or path covers the changed surface' introduces an undefined second disjunct. The module arm is fully mechanized (coverage map, registry confirmation, recorded ID); 'path' is defined nowhere and carries no recording obligation, so it can always be asserted — discharging the mid-iteration full-suite obligation and, in implement.md, the Devflow Reflection bullet that is the entire audit trail for missing coverage.

The planted-defect control's header comment claims the two assertions above it are clean-tree checks that cannot distinguish a verified clean tree from a guard reporting nothing. The second of those is the unit test, which carries planted-defect arms over synthetic fixtures — and the same block's sibling comment fifteen lines above says exactly that, so the block self-contradicts. The inventory's parallel claim that the other four covered guards have no planted-failure arm of their own is false in the same way.

The inventory's #556 exclusion row says the block interleaves the unit run with prose pins over three agents/*.md files and a review phase file; at HEAD it binds two review phase files (phase-2-verification.md and phase-4-verdict.md). A transcribed count that rots on the next edit.

The #707 coupled-heading-invariant comment block is orphaned: the next statement begins a different rationale (the claim gate) and the three assertions that follow are the claim-gate pins, while the heading pins it actually documents sit ~35 lines further down. A maintainer reading top-down attributes the rationale to the wrong pins.

The #668 comment enumerates four AC1 properties (no exec bit, no PyYAML import, no gh call, no network call) but only three are asserted — 'no network call' has no pin. Separately, the gh-call regex was changed from \bgh \b to (^|[^a-zA-Z_])gh during what the changeset and inventory both describe as a pure move; the change is defensible for BSD portability but is an undocumented behavior delta inside a stated move.

Four of the five extracted guard families still have no positive control (disclosed in the inventory). The #528 banned-exec sweep is the highest-value candidate: it asserts its spelling list is non-empty and complete but never proves the counting half is live.

The cloud /devflow:implement tier no longer has an explicitly-stated final full-suite obligation in implement.md: the retired sentence was tier-agnostic, its replacement is scoped to local/interactive. The guarantee survives via the unscoped 'never the final completion gate' sentence and CLAUDE.md tier 2, so this is a readability gap rather than a hole. Also: implement.md states the no-covering-module rule twice in adjacent paragraphs with subtly different scope ('registered module' vs 'module or path'), and CONTRIBUTING.md has a stray short line-wrap.

shadow.outcome_2_promotion_withheld: Decide outcome 2 applies — the shadow surfaced six new Important Phase 3 findings under full reviewer coverage, so the loop has NOT converged and would normally promote them into iteration 3 (the cap is 5; iterations 1 and 2 are spent, so the cap is not the reason). Promotion was withheld solely because the operator directed the loop to stop after iteration 2 and not to start another iteration. This is an operator halt, not a convergence: the findings are unfixed and unreviewed.
shadow.over_grade_gate: Not run: it fires only before an actual outcome-2 promotion, which was withheld by the operator halt. Two of the six new Important findings were instead verified directly by the orchestrator (see orchestrator_verified) and are confirmed, not suspected over-grades; the remaining four are unverified single-source labels, two of them from the empirically over-grading silent-failure-hunter, and would require the gate's recorded technical evaluation before driving any promotion.
shadow.park_calibration_gate: Not run: it fires only before a Decide outcome-1 conclusion, which is unreachable on this pass (outcome 2, and prompt_addenda is not the attested "none").

notes: Terminal state: NOT a converged clean approve. The loop was stopped by operator request after this iteration, before the Step 2.6 shadow pass and before a third engine pass could confirm the iteration-2 fixes introduced no new defects — and iteration 2 established that iteration 1's fixes DID introduce three documented falsehoods, so that risk is live, not theoretical.

Verification tier: full. lib/test/run.sh 12252 passed, 0 failed, zero skips at a380df9c; run-module.sh harness-python-guards 31/31 and review-and-fix-contract 68/68; regenerate-artifacts exit 0; shellcheck and ruff clean. The push and the local full run were started together per the policy this PR ships, and the completion claim was made only after reading the local run's summary.
